summary: Frontend-only testing of new Print Channel Map feature (iteration 10). All 16 specified test points passed: open-print button '⎙ Map' renders next to Presets; clicking opens print-modal with print-host wrapper + print-area card; header shows 'AudioSystem DSP — Channel Map', config label 'DSP 16+16 Dante · 16 physical + 16 virtual per side', Printed timestamp and Version; print-trigger labeled '⎙ Print / Save PDF' (orange) calls window.print(); print-close closes modal. Outputs table has 14 expected columns and 32 rows with correct data-testid='print-out-row-{id}'. Inputs table has 5 columns and 32 rows. Default routing verified: out_phy_0='IN 1', out_phy_1='IN 2', out_phy_2='—'. Description edit (out-out_phy-0-description = 'Lead Vocal') correctly propagates to Purpose column in print modal. Read-only mode allows opening print modal. Regression: Channels/Meters/Matrix tabs and Presets modal still functional after using print modal.
critical_code_review_comments: ChannelMapPrint.jsx is clean and well-structured; build routing logic is correct and consistent between outputs (Routed From) and inputs (Routes To) views.

data-testid coverage is complete for all new interactive/informational elements (open-print, print-modal, print-area, print-trigger, print-close, print-outputs-table, print-inputs-table, print-out-row-{id}).
Minor: ChannelMapPrint formats Hz with Math.round but does not handle missing crossover safely if state shape ever changes — currently safe given dspDefaults guarantees the shape.
Minor: routingByOut only adds keys with non-empty names which is fine; the JSX uses optional chaining and falls back to '—' correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Print Map feature fully verified in iter 10. Description input testid pattern is 'out-out_phy-{idx}-description' (hyphen, NOT underscore between out_phy and idx). All 14 output table columns verified in order: #, Type, Name, Purpose / Description, Gain, Pan, Delay, HPF, LPF, EQ, Comp, Lim, Mute, Routed From. window.print() is a no-op in headless Chromium; tests stubbed window.print to assert it was invoked.
